assertions.title: Containers CRUD preserves execution-policy fields without requiring tool execution
observations: Cargo ran `containers_crud_preserves_execution_policy_fields_without_requiring_tool_execution` successfully.

Source inspection shows the create request preserves `name`, `expires_after.anchor=last_active_at`, `file_ids`, `memory_limit=4g`, allowlist `network_policy`, nested `domain_secrets`, a referenced skill, and an inline zipped skill source without flattening.
The same test verifies retrieve/list only depend on documented read fields and that delete accepts an empty-body 200 response returning unit output.

assertions.title: Container files support upload, metadata lookup, and binary content download
observations: Cargo ran `container_files_support_upload_metadata_lookup_and_binary_content_download` successfully.
The test proves both raw multipart upload and pre-existing `file_id` creation paths, with typed metadata covering `path`, `source`, and `bytes` on retrieve/list responses.
`content` requests send `Accept: application/binary` to `/v1/containers/{container_id}/files/{file_id}/content` and return raw bytes without JSON coercion.

assertions.title: Skills CRUD and versioning preserve default-version semantics and content access
observations: Cargo ran `skills_crud_and_versioning_preserve_default_latest_and_binary_content_access` successfully and left the ignored live entitlement smoke unexecuted on this mocked surface.

Source inspection shows skill create/retrieve/list/update/delete preserve `default_version` and `latest_version`, while version create/retrieve/list/delete preserve stable version ids and typed `skill.version` parsing.
Both `/v1/skills/{id}/content` and `/v1/skills/{id}/versions/{version}/content` are exercised as binary downloads with `Accept: application/binary`.

assertions.title: Video creation exposes an inspectable asynchronous job lifecycle
observations: Cargo ran `video_job_lifecycle_and_transforms_preserve_typed_ids_polling_and_character_flows` successfully and left the ignored live video smoke unexecuted on this mocked surface.

The test covers `videos.create`, `videos.create_and_poll`, retrieve, list, and delete, asserting typed `status`, `progress`, `expires_at`, and `remixed_from_video_id` fields while polling until the mocked job reaches `completed`.
The shared video fixture includes stable job objects with `error: null`, so the passing typed parses confirm the current async job resource shape is accepted throughout the lifecycle.

assertions.title: Video transforms, character endpoints, and downloads maintain ID chaining and binary semantics
observations: `video_job_lifecycle_and_transforms_preserve_typed_ids_polling_and_character_flows` proves `edit`, `extend`, `remix`, `create_character`, and `get_character`, preserving source-video or character identity across returned objects.

Source inspection shows both id-backed and upload-backed `edit` and `extend` requests use `multipart/form-data`, preserving nested `video[id]` or uploaded file parts instead of flattening them into JSON.
`download_content_preserves_variant_query_and_binary_semantics` proves default, `thumbnail`, and `spritesheet` downloads preserve `variant` query parameters and return raw binary bytes.

assertions.title: Containers Preserve Domain Secrets and Inline Skills While Remaining Entitlement-Aware
observations: `containers_crud_preserves_execution_policy_fields_without_requiring_tool_execution` proves nested `domain_secrets`, inline skills, referenced skills, and copied `file_ids` remain intact in the create payload.

The same cargo run reports `live_containers_smoke_records_entitlement_failures_explicitly` as ignored, so this mocked surface did not actually execute the entitlement-aware live skip/success branch named by the contract.
assertions.issues: The mocked cargo surface proves the request-shaping half of the assertion, but it does not execute the ignored live entitlement smoke needed to observe an explicit entitlement-based skip or success outcome.

assertions.title: Container File Deletion Preserves Route and Result Semantics
observations: Cargo ran `delete_semantics_surface_typed_not_found_and_permission_errors` successfully.

Source inspection shows `containers.files.delete(container_id, file_id)` rejects blank ids locally, uses the documented `/v1/containers/{container_id}/files/{file_id}` route, and parses typed deletion results.
The same test also proves typed 404 not-found and 403 permission-denied API errors without requiring a follow-up list call.

assertions.title: Video Creation Preserves Multipart Request Shape and Current Create Parameters
observations: Cargo ran `create_request_shape_preserves_nested_reference_configuration_without_flattening` successfully.

Source inspection shows `videos.create` posts `multipart/form-data` to `/v1/videos` and preserves `prompt`, nested `input_reference[file_id]`, nested `input_reference[image_url]`, `model`, `seconds`, and `size` fields without flattening.
The response is parsed back into a typed asynchronous video job resource, matching the contract's current create-parameter shape.

commands.observation: The `containers_contract` binary finished successfully with 1 passed test (`containers_crud_preserves_execution_policy_fields_without_requiring_tool_execution`) and 1 ignored live smoke (`live_containers_smoke_records_entitlement_failures_explicitly`).

The `container_files_contract` binary finished successfully with 2 passed tests: `container_files_support_upload_metadata_lookup_and_binary_content_download` and `delete_semantics_surface_typed_not_found_and_permission_errors`.

The `skills_contract` binary finished successfully with 1 passed test (`skills_crud_and_versioning_preserve_default_latest_and_binary_content_access`) and 1 ignored live smoke (`live_skills_smoke_skips_explicitly_when_the_project_lacks_entitlement`).

The `videos_contract` binary finished successfully with 2 passed tests (`create_request_shape_preserves_nested_reference_configuration_without_flattening`, `video_job_lifecycle_and_transforms_preserve_typed_ids_polling_and_character_flows`) and 1 ignored live smoke (`live_videos_smoke_skips_explicitly_when_the_project_lacks_entitlement`).
The `video_downloads_contract` binary finished successfully with 1 passed test: `download_content_preserves_variant_query_and_binary_semantics`.

frictions.description: Cargo output named the passing test functions but did not expose the internal request-shape assertions, multipart field checks, or binary-header expectations needed to map each pass back to the validation contract.
frictions.resolution: Inspected the assigned Rust test sources (`tests/containers_contract.rs`, `tests/container_files_contract.rs`, `tests/skills_contract.rs`, `tests/videos_contract.rs`, and `tests/video_downloads_contract.rs`) to tie each passing test to the contract language.

blockers.description: The assigned mocked cargo surface does not execute the ignored live container entitlement smoke, so the entitlement-aware branch of the containers assertion remains unobserved in this flow.
blockers.quickFixAttempted: Confirmed from cargo output and source inspection that `live_containers_smoke_records_entitlement_failures_explicitly` exists and would record an explicit entitlement skip or success, but did not run it because this assignment is restricted to mocked cargo validation.

summary: Ran all 5 assigned mocked cargo commands successfully. Confirmed 7 assertions passed (VAL-ADV-008, 009, 010, 011, 012, 016, 017) and marked VAL-ADV-015 blocked because the mocked surface does not execute the ignored entitlement-aware live container smoke.
